schemas.name: abgeordnetenwatch
schemas.description: Die Beschreibung ist konkret und nennt klar Zielgruppe (deutsche Politiker), Datentypen (Profile, Parlamentsdaten, Mandate, Kandidaturen, Abstimmungen) sowie Quelle und Lizenz (Abgeordnetenwatch, CC0). Es fehlt allerdings ein expliziter Use-Case-Trigger (wann statt eines anderen Tools zu nutzen) und eine Abgrenzung, was die API nicht abdeckt.

schemas.name: fearAndGreed
schemas.description: The description clearly states the data source (alternative.me Crypto Fear and Greed Index) and lists three concrete capabilities: current reading, historical data with configurable lookback, and trend analysis. It is specific and concise, though it lacks explicit trigger phrases or example user queries that would push it to exemplary.

schemas.name: aqicn
schemas.description: Die Beschreibung ist klar und spezifisch: nennt den Datentyp (Real-time air quality), Skalierung (10,000+ stations), konkrete Metriken (AQI, PM2.5, PM10, O3, NO2, SO2, CO) und Zusatz (health recommendations). Kein dediziertes skill whenToUse vorhanden, was Use-Case-Triggering leicht einschraenkt; die Beschreibung selbst bleibt aber praezise.

schemas.name: jobs
schemas.description: Beschreibung nennt klar Anbieter, Domaene (Job-Suche) und vier Filter-Achsen (profession, location, employer, job type). Es fehlen explizite Use-Case-Kriterien oder Abgrenzungen.

schemas.name: jobsuche
schemas.description: Klar, benennt Quelle (Bundesagentur fuer Arbeit) und Zweck. Es fehlt expliziter whenToUse-Hinweis und konkrete Use-Cases oder Abgrenzungen.

schemas.name: professions
schemas.description: Klar: nennt Anbieter, BERUFENET, Umfang (3500+) und Geographie. Es fehlt dedizierter whenToUse-Block mit Use-Cases.

schemas.name: education
schemas.description: Die Beschreibung nennt den Anbieter (German Federal Employment Agency), den Inhalt (apprenticeship and training offers), die Region (Germany) und erwaehnt Filteroptionen. Es fehlen jedoch konkrete Use-Case-Hinweise und Abgrenzungen zu aehnlichen Tools, weshalb sie zwar verstaendlich, aber nicht exemplarisch spezifisch ist.

schemas.name: art-institute-chicago
schemas.description: Klar und spezifisch: nennt Datenbestand (130000+ Artworks), Suchdimensionen, Output-Eigenschaften und Zugangsbedingungen. Klare Domaenenabgrenzung.

schemas.name: traffic
schemas.description: Praezise: Domain (deutsche Autobahnen), Datentyp (Echtzeit-Verkehr), fuenf konkrete Datentypen. Es fehlt expliziter whenToUse-Block mit Trigger-Faellen.

schemas.name: metrics-api
schemas.description: Description is concrete and lists key metric categories (staking, throughput, gas, TPS, validators), though missing explicit triggers for when an LLM should select this tool.

schemas.name: avnu
schemas.description: Benennt klar Funktionalitaet, Plattform (Starknet) und Aggregator. Es fehlt explizite whenToUse-Anweisung mit Anwendungsfaellen.

schemas.name: schulen
schemas.description: Klar: Datenquelle (Berlin WFS GeoJSON), Inhalt, Zugangsbedingung. Spezifisch genug fuer LLM-Routing. Use-Cases koennten klarer sein.

schemas.name: events
schemas.description: Description clearly identifies the data domain (Berlin events: markets, festivals, public assemblies) and is specific enough to differentiate from other event sources.

schemas.name: procurement
schemas.description: Benennt klar Quelle (Berlin Vergabeplattform), Format (RSS feed), Inhalt. Es fehlt expliziter whenToUse-Hinweis, Aktualisierungsfrequenz, Geltungsbereich.

schemas.name: wfs-locations
schemas.description: Description is specific (Berlin geo data: dog parks, BBQ areas) and identifies the technology (WFS). Could be slightly broader with more example use cases but is clear.

schemas.name: bfsodl
schemas.description: Very specific description with concrete data source (BfS), exact scope (gamma dose rate, ~1,700 stations), frequency (10 min), and geographic coverage (Germany). Excellent triggerability.

schemas.name: bicscan
schemas.description: Clear, specific use case (risk scoring + portfolio for wallet addresses), names provider, and lists concrete outputs (risk ratings, token details). Very triggerable.

schemas.name: biorxiv
schemas.description: Description names specific data sources (bioRxiv, medRxiv), data type (preprint metadata), and lists clear categories (content, publication, publisher, statistics). Excellent triggerability.

schemas.name: bisStatistics
schemas.description: Description names provider (BIS), protocol (SDMX REST), and lists 7 concrete data domains (policy rates, exchange rates, credit gaps, property prices, CPI, debt securities, USD rates). Excellent specificity.

schemas.name: mina-mainnet
schemas.description: Description identifies blockchain (Mina) and scope (blocks, accounts, ZkApps) but is brief. The parenthetical 'working endpoints only' is an internal note that doesn't help LLM triggering.

schemas.name: utxoAndBlocks
schemas.description: Description is clear about scope (Bitcoin UTXOs and block stats) and provider, but brief. Could elaborate on use cases (e.g., wallet balance checking, blockchain forensics).

schemas.name: boldSystems
schemas.description: Names full provider (Barcode of Life Data Systems / BOLD), data scope (DNA barcode records), and search dimensions (taxonomy, geography, BIN). Highly specific and triggerable.

schemas.name: bonfida
schemas.description: Description is concrete: names data domain (.sol/SNS), 3 operations (resolve, favorite, list owned), and the underlying SDK. Highly triggerable for Solana wallet/domain questions.

schemas.name: bridgerates
schemas.description: Description names provider (LiFi) and core capabilities (bridge quotes, chains, tools, transfer data). Specific to cross-chain bridging domain, clearly triggerable.

schemas.name: bright-sky
schemas.description: Description is very specific: names data source (DWD, German weather agency), geographic scope (Germany), and explicit content (forecasts, current conditions, alerts, rainfall radar).

schemas.name: bsbMdz
schemas.description: Description names institution (Bayerische Staatsbibliothek MDZ), volume (3.16M+ items), protocol (IIIF), and content categories (manuscripts, prints, maps, music). Very specific.

schemas.name: bundesbankStatistics
schemas.description: Names institution (Deutsche Bundesbank), protocol (SDMX REST), and 4 specific data categories (exchange rates, monetary stats, financial market, macroeconomic). Highly triggerable.

schemas.name: budget
schemas.description: Exemplary description: names data domain (German federal budget), explicit year range (2012-2025), 3 classification dimensions (Einzelplan, function, group), and capability (hierarchical drill-down).

schemas.name: bundeswahlleiterin
schemas.description: Exemplary: names institution (Die Bundeswahlleiterin), specific election (Bundestagswahl 2025), all query dimensions, both vote types (Erst/Zweit), constituency count (299), and access (free, no key).

schemas.name: catalogueoflife
schemas.description: Names data source (Catalogue of Life ChecklistBank), scope (taxonomic names), volume (2.07M+ species), and coverage (all kingdoms). Very specific.

schemas.name: cern-open-data
schemas.description: Exemplary: names institution (CERN), domain (particle physics), volume (50,000+ records), specific experiments (ATLAS, CMS, LHCb, ALICE), data types, and notable use cases (Higgs boson). No API key noted.

schemas.name: price-feeds
schemas.description: Description names provider (Chainlink), scope (oracle price feeds), chain count (13 EVM), discovery flow (chains → pairs → prices), and infrastructure (Infura RPC). Very specific.

schemas.name: price-feeds-multicall
schemas.description: Clear distinction from individual feed schema: emphasizes batch reading via Multicall3 for efficiency. Names the chain scope and what's returned.

schemas.name: chainlist
schemas.description: Names provider (Chainlist), scope (EVM chain metadata), and concrete data types returned (chain IDs, RPC, explorers, WebSocket, native currency). Highly triggerable for blockchain config lookup.

schemas.name: tradingview-charts
schemas.description: Description identifies provider (TradingView) and output (chart screenshots), but is brief and lacks specific use cases (e.g., stock vs crypto, technical analysis). Acceptable but not exemplary.

schemas.name: VegaLiteCharts
schemas.description: Description is clear: names tech (Vega-Lite), chart types (candlestick/line/multi-line/bar), output format (base64 PNG), and key feature (local rendering, no external API).

schemas.name: cheapshark
schemas.description: Klar formuliert, nennt Use-Case (Game Deals), konkrete Stores, Filter-Optionen und API-Key-Freiheit; sehr spezifisch und actionable.

schemas.name: chembl
schemas.description: Beschreibt Inhalt (2.4M+ Molekuele), Datenarten und Quelle EBI klar; koennte konkrete Use-Cases noch staerker benennen.

schemas.name: clinicaltrialsgov
schemas.description: Beschreibung ist klar und nennt Datenarten, bleibt aber relativ kurz ohne konkrete Use-Cases oder Beispielanfragen.

schemas.name: cocktaildb
schemas.description: Sehr spezifisch: nennt 600+ Cocktails, Suchmoeglichkeiten, Datenfelder und API-Key-Hinweis; sehr klar.

schemas.name: discovery
schemas.description: Kurze Beschreibung nennt x402 und Coinbase Bazaar, aber bleibt vage zu Inhalt, Filtern und konkretem Anwendungsfall.

schemas.name: markets
schemas.description: Beschreibung nennt klar trading pairs, Datenarten und Filterungsmoglichkeiten.

schemas.name: assets
schemas.description: Klar und prazise, beschreibt Datenarten (prices, market caps, supply, volume, historical) und Abdeckung (thousands of tokens).

schemas.name: exchanges
schemas.description: Klar formuliert, nennt zwei Operationen (list + get by ID) und Datentypen (volume rankings, metadata).

schemas.name: getCategories
schemas.description: Klar und spezifisch, nennt zwei Operationen und konkrete Metriken (market cap, volume, change) per Category.

schemas.name: global
schemas.description: Spezifisch und klar: nennt konkrete Metriken (market cap, volume, BTC/ETH dominance) und DeFi-spezifische Daten.

schemas.name: trending
schemas.description: Klar und spezifisch: nennt drei Datenarten (coins, NFTs, categories) und enthaltene Metriken (price, volume).

schemas.name: coingecko-stablecoins
schemas.description: Klar und spezifisch: nennt Stablecoins, market metrics, peg stability und historische Trends - sehr fokussierter Use-Case.

schemas.name: search-ohlc
schemas.description: Spezifisch: nennt zwei Operationen (search + OHLC) und Use-Case (technical analysis), API-Tier-Hinweis enthalten.

schemas.name: derivatives
schemas.description: Klar formuliert, beschreibt drei Operationen (list, get by ID, products per exchange) und das Datenthema (derivatives).

schemas.name: coins
schemas.description: Sehr knappe Beschreibung listet die Endpunkte auf, nennt aber keinen konkreten Use-Case und gibt wenig Kontext zum Inhalt.

schemas.name: category
schemas.description: Exemplarisch: nennt alle 5 Operationen, Workflow-Beziehungen (getCategories -> getCategory; getIdMap -> getMetadata/getQuotes) und ist sehr handlungsleitend.

schemas.name: fear-and-greed
schemas.description: Exemplarisch klar: nennt Use-Case (market psychology), beide Operationen mit Workflow und Skala-Hinweis explizit.

schemas.name: listings
schemas.description: Klar und specific: nennt paginated list, Filter (price, market cap, volume, supply), sort by any major metric, und convert-Parameter.

schemas.name: cmc-index
schemas.description: Klar und spezifisch: erklaert CMC100-Benchmark, beide Operationen und nennt Use-Case (current value vs time-series).

schemas.name: exchanges
schemas.description: Klar formuliert, nennt drei Operationen (list, info, market pairs - allerdings nur 2 echte exchange-Tools + 2 weitere); enthaelt etwas Inkonsistenz (4 Tools im Schema, Description erwaehnt nur exchanges).

schemas.name: copernicusland
schemas.description: Exemplarisch: nennt konkrete Datenarten (Urban Atlas, CORINE, etc.), Abdeckung (Europa), API-Key-Freiheit, und Workflow (listProducts -> datasetsByProduct -> getDatasetDetail).

schemas.name: core
schemas.description: Exemplarisch: 200M+ Papers, klare Datenarten (works, outputs, providers, journals), Workflow zwischen Tools explizit (searchWorks -> getWork -> discoverFulltext).

schemas.name: cosmos-bank
schemas.description: Exemplarisch: nennt konkret abgedeckte Module (bank, staking, governance), Adress-Format-Hinweis, Tool-Verkettung (getBalances -> getDelegations + getValidators).

schemas.name: crossref
schemas.description: Klar und konkret: 150M+ DOI Records, nennt alle Datenarten (works, citations, funders, journals, members) und polite-pool-Hinweis.

schemas.name: currencies
schemas.description: Concise, specific description mentioning provider, free-tier limit, and all four endpoint purposes.

schemas.name: analytics
schemas.description: Exemplary description: lists scope, names each tool's purpose, and clarifies cross-references between getVolumes/getAllGauges pool addresses.

schemas.name: crvusd
schemas.description: Clear and focused description, lists exactly what data is provided (CRV supply, crvUSD supply, AMM volumes).

schemas.name: pools
schemas.description: Specific description mentioning 21 EVM chains and the data types (TVL, coins, gauge info, etc.); concise and informative.

schemas.name: dailymed
schemas.description: Concise and specific: lists data types (FDA labels, SPL, NDC, drug classes) and the source (NLM DailyMed REST).

schemas.name: statistics
schemas.description: Identifies source (DESTATIS) and concrete categories (economic indicators, labour, energy, trade, financial markets); no period but otherwise clear.

schemas.name: datacite
schemas.description: Clear scope (DOI metadata) and concrete numbers (60M+ DOIs, 3000+ repositories); identifies content types.

schemas.name: dataEuropaEu
schemas.description: Exemplary description with scale (1.6M+ datasets, 36 countries, 200+ catalogues), filter dimensions, and accessible metadata listed.

schemas.name: datamuse
schemas.description: Exemplary: clearly identifies domain, use cases (writers, crossword solvers, language learners), scale (100k+ words), and access (free, no key).

schemas.name: datetimeUtils
schemas.description: Clear scope (current time, arithmetic, formatting), notes local computation, and lists realistic use cases (travel, schedules).

schemas.name: dawum
schemas.description: Concise, specific description naming source (dawum.de) and content (German election polls, institutes, parliaments).

schemas.name: dblp
schemas.description: Specific scope (CS bibliography), scale (6M+ publications), and three distinct search modes (papers, authors, venues) called out.

schemas.name: debridge
schemas.description: Concise description listing capabilities (bridge quotes, chains, tokens, orders) and naming the protocol (DeBridge DLN).

schemas.name: yields
schemas.description: Specific scope (yields, APY, TVL, rewards) with two clear capabilities; identifies source.

schemas.name: coins
schemas.description: Mentions DeFi Llama and 'batch query ERC20 and native token prices' but actual schema only exposes single-token coingecko price lookups, slight overstatement.

schemas.name: catalogue
schemas.description: Specific source (DESTATIS GENESIS) and resource types (tables, statistics, variables, metadata); concise.

schemas.name: deutschedigitalebibliothek
schemas.description: Specific scope (40M+ digitized objects), sources (museums, archives, libraries, monuments), and country named explicitly.

schemas.name: defiPrices
schemas.description: Lists data scope (DEX prices, pools, liquidity, transactions) and identifies source; functional but unspecific in trigger phrasing.

schemas.name: boosted
schemas.description: Specific domain (boosted tokens on DexScreener), names two distinct endpoints (latest, most active) and scope (across all DEX chains).

schemas.name: tokenInfo
schemas.description: Specific scope listing four endpoints (profiles, search, token-to-pair, pools) and source (DexScreener).

schemas.name: pairs
schemas.description: Mentions provider and capabilities (pair lookup, order checks) but somewhat terse and uses vague 'buy/sell order checks' phrasing.

schemas.name: tokenpairs
schemas.description: Generic description listing data types (pair data, price, metrics) and source; lacks named endpoint capabilities or specific scale.

schemas.name: documents
schemas.description: Clear specific scope (Drucksachen, Plenarprotokolle) with German terminology preserved; identifies source DIP API and parliament.

schemas.name: proceedings
schemas.description: Clear description naming the DIP API, listing the four resource types (Vorgaenge, Vorgangspositionen, Aktivitaeten, Personen) with German+English glosses. Could mention typical use cases or filters more explicitly.

schemas.name: discogs
schemas.description: Highly specific description with concrete scale numbers (19M+ releases, 9.9M+ artists), names the API purpose and supported entities precisely.

schemas.name: disease-sh
schemas.description: Excellent description: scope (200+ countries), data sources (Johns Hopkins, Worldometers), refresh interval (10 min), and access requirement (no API key). Highly actionable.

schemas.name: dnd-api
schemas.description: Very clear description with concrete content numbers (320+ spells, 300+ monsters, 12 classes, 9 races), the SRD scope, edition (5e), and no-API-key requirement.

schemas.name: doaj
schemas.description: Clear description with concrete scale (20,000+ journals, 10M+ articles), scope (peer-reviewed, open access), and disciplines (all). Could mention API key requirement or rate limits.

schemas.name: dottEscooter
schemas.description: Excellent: precise (GBFS v2.3), scope (125+ cities), data types (real-time availability, pricing, geofencing), and no-API-key noted.

schemas.name: dpla
schemas.description: Clear with concrete scale (15M+ items) and content types listed (books, photographs, maps, audio, video) plus sources (libraries, archives, museums).

schemas.name: duckduckgo
schemas.description: Clear description listing the response types (instant answers, abstracts, related topics, definitions) and noting no API key. Could mention limitations like no general web search.

schemas.name: getResults
schemas.description: Clear and focused description naming Dune Analytics, the function (fetch latest cached result by query ID), and the return type. Compact, single-purpose schema.

schemas.name: weather
schemas.description: Brief description naming DWD (Deutscher Wetterdienst) and WarnWetter API. Could specify what data is returned (forecasts, warnings), coverage, or freshness.

schemas.name: dwds
schemas.description: Clear description listing functions (definitions, frequencies, IPA, snippets) and naming the DWDS source. Specific and actionable.

schemas.name: ebird
schemas.description: Clear and concise: names the source (Cornell Lab), data types (recent/notable sightings, hotspots), and global scope. Could mention API key requirement.

schemas.name: ecbSdmx
schemas.description: Clear description naming the ECB SDMX REST API and listing covered data types (exchange rates, interest rates, monetary aggregates). Mentions time series filtering.

schemas.name: counters
schemas.description: Clear description naming Eco-Counter, data type (bike/pedestrian counts), and scope (German cities + worldwide). Could mention freshness or auth requirements.

schemas.name: eeaWaterbase
schemas.description: Excellent: names DISCODATA SQL-to-JSON, covered datasets (CO2, EUNIS biodiversity, GHG policies), query language (T-SQL), and no-key requirement.

schemas.name: emscSeismic
schemas.description: Clear: names EMSC and FDSN, lists filter dimensions (magnitude, location, depth, time). Could mention data freshness or coverage area.

schemas.name: ens-public
schemas.description: Clear and compact: lists all features (resolve, reverse, text records, avatars, content hashes) and says no API key. Could mention which RPC and which chain (Ethereum mainnet) explicitly.

schemas.name: ens-resolution
schemas.description: Reasonable scope (ENS plus some EVM chains via ethers.js, forward and reverse). 'some EVM name services' is vague — listing supported chains in the description would help.

schemas.name: enso
schemas.description: Exemplary: names Enso Finance, describes purpose (intent-based DeFi routing), lists all capabilities, and gives the discovery flow (getNetworks -> chainId, getProtocols -> slugs feed into getTokens).

schemas.name: epoLinkedData
schemas.description: Strong: names EPO Linked Data API (ELDA), lists covered entities (applications, publications, families, classifications), gives scale (100M+ documents), and no-API-key requirement.

schemas.name: ergast-f1
schemas.description: Excellent: names Ergast API and its Jolpi hosting, lists covered data (races, standings, circuits, schedules), gives scale (1,000+ races, 850+ drivers, 200+ constructors), date range, and no-API-key.

schemas.name: signature-utils
schemas.description: Beschreibung ist praezise: nennt vier konkrete Operationen (verify personal/EIP-712, hash, recover) und stellt klar, dass kein RPC/API-Key noetig ist. Kontext fuer Anwendungsfall ist eindeutig.

schemas.name: convert-utils
schemas.description: Clear and specific: lists all conversion utilities (wei/ether, hexlify, UTF-8), notes ethers.js source and offline use.

schemas.name: hash-address
schemas.description: Clear and specific: lists all utilities (keccak256, Solidity packed, CREATE2/CREATE, ENS namehash, EIP-55 checksum), notes ethers.js source and offline use.

schemas.name: abi-utils
schemas.description: Clear and specific: lists all six utilities (decode/encode calldata, decode events, compute selectors, ABI en/decode), notes offline (no RPC/API key).

schemas.name: getContractEthereum
schemas.description: Beschreibung spezifiziert genau: ABI + Source Code, Ethereum Mainnet, fuer verifizierte Contracts. Sehr klar und fokussiert.

schemas.name: account
schemas.description: Beschreibung nennt die Hauptfunktionen (balances, txs, internal txs, ERC20, logs) und benennt die API-Version klar. Kontext klar, Domaene eindeutig.

schemas.name: getGaspriceMultichain
schemas.description: Beschreibung praezise: Gas-Oracle (safe/proposed/fast) ueber multiple EVM-Chains. Explizit unterstuetzte Chains genannt. Klar fokussiert.

schemas.name: getContractMultichain
schemas.description: Beschreibung klar: ABI + Source ueber 60+ EVM-Chains. Erwaehnt Listen-Funktion und Lookup-by-Address. Kontext eindeutig.

schemas.name: ethscriptions-api-part2
schemas.description: Beschreibung erlaeutert Domaene (Ethscriptions, Ethereum-Artefakte) und benennt unterstuetzte Operationen (list, search, inspect) sowie API-Version. Klar fokussiert.

schemas.name: europeana
schemas.description: Beschreibung praezise: kulturelles Erbe aus europaeischen Museen, Bibliotheken, Archiven, Galerien ueber Europeana API. Klar und thematisch fokussiert.

schemas.name: europepmc
schemas.description: Beschreibung sehr klar: 47M+ Life-Science Publikationen, mit konkreten Anwendungsfaellen (citations, references, full-text, grants, annotations). Praezise und numerisch belegt.

schemas.name: eu-safety-gate
schemas.description: Beschreibung exzellent: erklaert was Safety Gate ist (ex-RAPEX), Scope (EEA), Inhalt (alerts/risk/categories), Skalen (250+/20+/30+), und Hinweis auf 'free, no API key'. Sehr klar.

schemas.name: familySearch
schemas.description: Beschreibung klar: FamilySearch Places API fuer genealogische Ortsdaten, Jurisdiktionen, Place-Types. Fokus auf historische und moderne Locations gut erkennbar.

schemas.name: holidays
schemas.description: Beschreibung klar fokussiert: deutsche Feiertage, alle Bundeslaender, optionales Filtering. Knapp aber praezise.

schemas.name: flixbus
schemas.description: Beschreibung praezise: FlixBus + FlixTrain, autocomplete (cities/stations), trip search mit Echtzeitpreisen, Skope (Europa), no auth. Komplette Information.

schemas.name: footballdata
schemas.description: Beschreibung konkret: 183 Wettbewerbe weltweit, mit konkreten Datentypen (matches, standings, teams, scorers, players). Praezise und numerisch belegt.

schemas.name: frankfurter
schemas.description: Beschreibung exzellent: ECB Quelle, 30+ Currencies, latest+historical+timeseries, Update-Zeit (16:00 CET), 'free, no API key'. Komplett und klar.

schemas.name: fred
schemas.description: Beschreibung gut: FRED (St. Louis Fed), Inhaltsumfang (GDP, employment, inflation), klare Anwendungsdomaene (Wirtschaftsdaten).

schemas.name: free-dictionary
schemas.description: Beschreibung klar: englische Woerterbuch-API mit Definitions, Phonetik, Synonyme, Antonyme, Beispiele. Spezifisch und fokussiert.

schemas.name: gbif
schemas.description: Beschreibung praezise: GBIF mit 2+ Mrd Records, Anwendung (species, names, occurrence). Domaene und Skala klar.

schemas.name: gdacsDisasterAlerts
schemas.description: Beschreibung klar: GDACS fuer Naturkatastrophen, Liste der Typen (earthquake, flood, cyclone, volcano, wildfire, drought), inkl. Alert-Levels und Geo-Daten.

schemas.name: gdelt
schemas.description: Beschreibung exzellent: GDELT, 100+ Sprachen, 15-min Updates, 300M+ Artikel seit 2015, frei, Rate-Limit explizit. Komplett.

schemas.name: geocoding
schemas.description: Beschreibung knapp aber komplett: forward + reverse + autocomplete. Klare Anwendungsfaelle.

schemas.name: geonames
schemas.description: Beschreibung praezise: 11M+ Namen, Postleitzahlen, Elevation, Timezones. Konkrete Funktionen (search, reverse, country info) klar.

schemas.name: law-index
schemas.description: Beschreibung praezise: gesetze-im-internet.de Index, 6,800+ Gesetze, mit Abkuerzung, Titel, Link. Klare Abgrenzung zum content-Schema.

schemas.name: law-content
schemas.description: Beschreibung praezise: Lesen+Suchen in deutschen Bundesgesetzen, mit konkreten Funktionen (paragraphs, full-text, TOC, structure).

schemas.name: lil-nouns
schemas.description: Beschreibung klar: Lil Nouns DAO Governance-Proposals via Goldsky Subgraph. Beide Operationen (list/byId) explizit.

schemas.name: googleFactCheck
schemas.description: Beschreibung klar: Google Fact Check Tools API, Text+Image Suche, ClaimReviews mit Ratings und Quellen. Anwendungsfall offensichtlich.

schemas.name: sparql-analytics
schemas.description: Description is specific (DCAT-AP.de, SPARQL aggregate analytics) and lists concrete capabilities (themes, publishers, formats, keywords, recent). Quantification of 177k+ entries adds clarity.

schemas.name: greix
schemas.description: Excellent specificity: source (ECONtribute), exact time range (1963-2024), city count (27), property types covered, granularity levels, and pricing model (free, no key). Clear scope.

schemas.name: gutendex
schemas.description: Clear scope (Project Gutenberg, 70k+ ebooks) and explicit filter dimensions named (author, language, subject, downloads). Slightly terse but unambiguous.

schemas.name: hamburgUdp
schemas.description: Concrete scope: OGC SensorThings API, Hamburg, named sub-domains (E-charging, StadtRad, traffic, energy). Mentions OData features and no-API-key constraint.

schemas.name: hathitrust
schemas.description: Specific scope (HathiTrust, 17M+ volumes), explicit identifier types (ISBN, OCLC, LCCN) and source (research libraries). Clear and actionable.

schemas.name: hebcal
schemas.description: Clear functional scope (holidays, Shabbat times, conversion), enumerates which event classes are covered (major/minor/fast days/Torah readings), and notes free access.

schemas.name: jsonFeeds
schemas.description: Concise and clear scope (Hacker News, JSON feeds, list of feed kinds) but somewhat terse. Doesn't mention filters/quotas.

schemas.name: flood-monitoring
schemas.description: Very specific: source (LHP), data type (real-time flood), quantification (~1,200 stations), geographic scope (16 federal states), and two output types (current readings + warnings).

schemas.name: honeypot
schemas.description: Clear use-case (detect honeypot tokens), specific signals checked (buy/sell tax, liquidity, contract risks), and target (EVM token address). Concrete API source named.

schemas.name: hudoc
schemas.description: Specific source (HUDOC), quantified scale (90K+ docs), explicit doc types (judgments, decisions, communications) and time range (since 1959). ECHR scope crystal clear.

schemas.name: imfdatamapper
schemas.description: Clear scope: IMF DataMapper, quantification (120+ indicators, 190+ countries), domain examples (GDP, inflation, debt, trade), and free access noted.

schemas.name: inaturalist
schemas.description: Clear and specific: iNaturalist citizen science, lists primary use cases (observations, taxa, places, counts). Implicit but evident scope.

schemas.name: chart-generator
schemas.description: Concise and specific (candlestick PNG, OHLCV, ECharts, base64 output for Markdown embedding). Slightly narrow; doesn't mention indicator overlays explicitly outside parameters.

schemas.name: indicatorts-schema-part5
schemas.description: Clear and informative: explicit library name, 80+ indicators, named categories (momentum, trend, volume) with examples, and local computation. Slightly generic per-part description (same for all parts).

schemas.name: indicatorts-schema-part6
schemas.description: Clear and informative: explicit library, 80+ indicators, named categories with examples, local computation. Generic per-part description shared with sibling parts.

schemas.name: indicatorts-schema-part7
schemas.description: Clear and informative: explicit library, 80+ indicators, named categories with examples, local computation. Generic per-part description shared with sibling parts.

schemas.name: indicatorts-schema-part8
schemas.description: Clear and informative: explicit library, 80+ indicators, named categories with examples, local computation. Generic per-part description shared with sibling parts.

schemas.name: indicatorts-schema-part9
schemas.description: Clear and informative: explicit library, 80+ indicators, named categories with examples, local computation. Generic per-part description shared with sibling parts.

schemas.name: infravelo
schemas.description: Very specific: source (infraVelo), city (Berlin), domain (cycling infrastructure), data elements (bike lanes, parking, traffic signals), output format (GeoJSON), and license noted.

schemas.name: node-read-part1
schemas.description: Excellent specificity: source (Infura JSON-RPC), full chain list enumerated (Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism, Base, BSC, Avalanche, Linea, Scroll, zkSync, Mantle, Celo), and concrete read capabilities listed (blocks, balances, transactions, receipts, code, gas, logs).

schemas.name: node-read-part2
schemas.description: Excellent specificity: same shared description as part1 — Infura JSON-RPC across 12 named EVM chains with concrete capability list including event logs (relevant for this part).

schemas.name: contract-read
schemas.description: Clear scope: read-only contract calls via Infura on EVM mainnets, ABI-based signature. Slightly terse on which exact chains supported (deferred to enum).

schemas.name: insolvenzbekanntmachungen
schemas.description: Specific official source (insolvenzbekanntmachungen.de), domain (German insolvency), explicit search dimensions listed, and structured output noted.

schemas.name: internetArchive
schemas.description: Specific scope: 40M+ items, listed media types (books, audio, video, software, web pages), and capabilities (metadata, files, statistics). Doesn't mention Wayback explicitly though that's offered.

schemas.name: ipuParline
schemas.description: Clear source (IPU Parline), domain (parliamentary data), explicit entities (countries, parliaments, chambers, elections, specialized bodies), and scale (190+ countries).

schemas.name: irys
schemas.description: Klare Beschreibung der Datenquelle (Irys GraphQL, decentralized storage), nennt zentrale Filter (owner, token, tags, timestamp) und auth requirements. Etwas knapp, aber spezifisch genug.

schemas.name: tenders
schemas.description: Beschreibt klar Provider (German IT procurement RSS) und Kategorien (software/hardware/internet/telecom/consulting), aber keine Hinweise auf Sprache der Inhalte, Aktualisierungsfrequenz oder Anwendungsfaelle.

schemas.name: iucnRedList
schemas.description: Sehr spezifische Beschreibung: API-Version, Filteroptionen (taxonomy/country/habitat/threat), Skalierungs-Info (150k+ Arten). Klar abgegrenzter Use Case fuer Biodiversitaets-Queries.

schemas.name: jikan
schemas.description: Exzellente Beschreibung: Datenquelle (MyAnimeList), Skalierung (25k+ Anime, 60k+ Manga), gelieferte Felder, auth/rate limits. Vollstaendig und LLM-freundlich.

schemas.name: jplfireball
schemas.description: Konkrete Beschreibung der Datenquelle (NASA JPL fireball/bolide), nennt die wichtigsten Felder (date/location/energy/velocity) und Sensor-Provenienz (US Gov).

schemas.name: jplcad
schemas.description: Klarer Scope (JPL Close Approach Data), nennt Filterdimensionen (date range, distance, object type PHA/NEO/comet, designation, magnitude). Spezifisch genug fuer LLM-Auswahl.

schemas.name: jplsbdb
schemas.description: Klar abgegrenzt (Small-Body Database asteroids/comets), nennt die liefernden Datentypen (orbital elements, physical parameters, close approaches, alternate designations).

schemas.name: jplsentry
schemas.description: Klare Beschreibung: NASA JPL Sentry Impact Risk Assessment mit konkreten Datenpunkten (probability, Palermo scale, virtual impactors). Domain-spezifisch und eindeutig.

schemas.name: ladesaeulenregister
schemas.description: Spezifische Beschreibung mit Provider (Bundesnetzagentur), Skalierung (175k+ Stationen), Datentyp (ArcGIS FeatureServer), und Filteroptionen (location, city, state, operator, power).

schemas.name: ladestationen
schemas.description: Klare Beschreibung mit Provider (Bundesnetzagentur), Quelle (ArcGIS FeatureServer) und Scope (alle oeffentlichen Stationen Deutschland). Keine Skalierungsangabe wie beim Schwester-Schema.

schemas.name: lastfm
schemas.description: Klare Beschreibung der Datentypen (artist/track/album/charts/recommendations) und Quelle (Last.fm/listening data). Knapp, aber ausreichend spezifisch.

schemas.name: libraryOfCongress
schemas.description: Sehr konkret: Datentypen (books/photos/maps/manuscripts/audio/video), Skalierung (170M+ records). Klarer Mediendaten-Fokus.

schemas.name: lobid
schemas.description: Sehr spezifisch: Provider (hbz), Skalierung (250M+ holdings, GND), Datenfelder, Format (REST/JSON-LD). Klar abgegrenzter Use Case fuer deutsche Bibliotheksdaten.

schemas.name: search
schemas.description: Klare Beschreibung: full-text search ueber addresses/tx/blocks/tokens, mit redirect support. Passt exakt zu den zwei Tools.

schemas.name: sourceCode
schemas.description: Klare Aufzaehlung der vier Funktionen (list contracts, ABI, source code, creation tx). Spezifisch und matchend zu Tools.

schemas.name: stats
schemas.description: Beschreibung erwaehnt korrekt die drei Tools (stats overview, tx volume chart, market price chart) und Quelle (BlockScout). Klar und passend.

schemas.name: transactions
schemas.description: Klare Aufzaehlung der sieben Transaktions-Funktionen (list, details by hash, token transfers, internal txs, logs, raw traces, state changes). Passt zum Schema.

schemas.name: address-part1
schemas.description: Klare Beschreibung (LUKSO via BlockScout) mit Aufzaehlung der Datentypen (transactions, transfers, internal, logs, balances, history). Knapp, aber praezise.

schemas.name: blocks
schemas.description: Klare Beschreibung der vier Block-Funktionen (list/details/transactions/withdrawals) mit Quelle (LUKSO BlockScout). Spezifisch und kompakt.

schemas.name: nfts
schemas.description: Klar abgegrenzt: NFT-Daten auf LUKSO (collections, instances, holders, transfers). Passt zum Schema-Inhalt, Quelle (BlockScout) genannt.

schemas.name: tokens
schemas.description: Klare Auflistung der Token-Funktionen (list, details, transfer history, holders, counters). Passt zu den fuenf Tools.

schemas.name: magiceden
schemas.description: Clear, specific description naming the marketplace (Magic Eden), platform (Solana), and concrete data types (collection stats, listings, metadata, activity). Helps an LLM decide when to use the schema.

schemas.name: energy-units
schemas.description: Specific German energy registry (MaStR) with concrete dataset size (8.4M+), data types (electricity/gas generation/consumption), and core features (filtering, pagination). Clear scope.

schemas.name: servers
schemas.description: Clear purpose (browse/search MCP server registry) but description is brief and does not detail filtering capabilities or scale. Adequate for routing decisions.

schemas.name: mealdb
schemas.description: Highly descriptive: dataset (TheMealDB, 300+ meals, world cuisines), capabilities (search, filter, browse), output features (images, video tutorials, instructions), and API key tier info. Excellent specificity.

schemas.name: rss-feeds
schemas.description: Clear scope: Medium articles via RSS, four entry points (tag, user, publication, topic). Mentions metadata/summaries. Adequate specificity.

schemas.name: merkl
schemas.description: Specific: DeFi rewards on 50+ chains, concrete capabilities (campaigns with APR/TVL, user rewards, chain metadata), named integrations (Uniswap, Aave, Velodrome). Good routing signal.

schemas.name: metaver
schemas.description: Very specific: 31,900+ German metadata records, 8 federal states, CSW 2.0.2 protocol, download formats (WFS/WMS/CSV/GeoJSON), free, no API key. Excellent routing info.

schemas.name: meteora
schemas.description: Specific: Meteora DLMM pools on Solana, with concrete data types (pools, pair groups, OHLCV, volume history, protocol metrics). Clear scope for liquidity/DEX analysis.

schemas.name: metMuseum
schemas.description: Excellent specificity: Met Museum, 470K+ artworks, search/filter capabilities, returned data (images, artist, provenance), CC0 licensing. Clear routing.

schemas.name: mobidataBw
schemas.description: Highly specific: Baden-Wuerttemberg mobility data, concrete dataset sizes (27,966 parking sites, 100+ GBFS feeds), distinct data types (parking, GTFS stops, sharing, roadworks), free, no key needed.

schemas.name: defiApi
schemas.description: Specific: DeFi portfolio analysis via Moralis with named protocols (Uniswap, Aave, Lido, MakerDAO, EigenLayer). Clear summary vs detail distinction.

schemas.name: priceApi
schemas.description: Specific: token/NFT price data via Moralis (ERC20 prices, NFT floor prices current/historical, sale history, DEX OHLCV). Good distinction from blockchain/wallet APIs.

schemas.name: blockchainApi
schemas.description: Clear scope: low-level blockchain data via Moralis (blocks, transactions, native history) across 30+ EVM chains. Good differentiation from other Moralis APIs.

schemas.name: walletApi-part1
schemas.description: Specific: wallet intelligence with concrete features (balance, history, net worth, profitability, active chain detection, ENS/Unstoppable resolution). Clear scope.

schemas.name: tokenApi-part2
schemas.description: Specific: ERC20 token analytics via Moralis with many concrete features named (metadata, holders, transfers, DEX swaps, sniper detection, liquidity, balances, approvals, rankings). Good differentiation.

schemas.name: nftApi-part1
schemas.description: Clear scope: comprehensive NFT data via Moralis (stats, metadata, ownership, transfers, trades, traits, rankings) across 30+ EVM chains. Adequate routing info.

schemas.name: markets
schemas.description: Specific: Morpho Blue lending markets via GraphQL with concrete capabilities (markets, positions, chains) and explicit chain support list (Ethereum, Base, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism).

schemas.name: marine-data
schemas.description: Description is clear and specific — names the institution (BfG, German Federal Institute of Hydrology), the domain (marine environment), and the data source (coastal states and research institutions). Strong signal for when an LLM should select this schema.

schemas.name: musicBrainz
schemas.description: Specific: MusicBrainz with scale (2M+ artists, 30M+ recordings), entity types (artists, recordings, releases, release groups), and lookup methods (MBID or keyword). Clear scope.

schemas.name: nager-date
schemas.description: Very specific: 100+ countries via Nager.Date with all features named (year+country, upcoming, long weekends, country list), free, no key. Excellent routing.

schemas.name: nasaapod
schemas.description: Specific: NASA APOD with concrete output (titles, explanations, high-res URLs) and start date (1995). Adequate scope.

schemas.name: nasaEonet
schemas.description: Very specific: NASA EONET with concrete event categories (wildfires, storms, volcanoes, earthquakes, floods, sea ice), worldwide scope, free, no key. Excellent routing.

schemas.name: nasaepic
schemas.description: Description is specific and clear — names the source satellite (DSCOVR), the camera (EPIC), data types (natural, enhanced, aerosol, cloud), and what is returned (metadata, coordinates). Slightly weakened by absence of explicit use-case framing.

schemas.name: nasaExoplanet
schemas.description: Description names the API (NASA Exoplanet Archive), the protocol (TAP/ADQL), dataset size (6100+), and lists searchable dimensions. Could mention typical use scenarios more explicitly.

schemas.name: nasaneows
schemas.description: Description clearly names the API (NeoWs) and three usage modes (close approach feed, lookup, browse). Brief but specific. Could mention the 7-day feed limit at description level.

schemas.name: nasatechport
schemas.description: Specific naming of the API (NASA TechPort) and listed entities (projects, programs, organizations, taxonomy) is clear. Slightly generic — could mention typical research/portfolio use cases.

schemas.name: neuris
schemas.description: Description is precise — names the official system (Rechtsinformationsportal), the operator (Federal Ministry of Justice), the data types (legislation, case law, legal documents) and the protocol (JSON REST). Bilingual context provided.

schemas.name: news
schemas.description: Description is short and accurate but generic — does not name the provider (NewsAPI) or scope (free vs paid, archive depth). Acceptable but not exemplary.

schemas.name: getNews
schemas.description: Names the provider and scope (crypto news) clearly but is short. Doesn't mention features like sentiment analysis/AI tags surfaced in the schema, which could help routing decisions.

schemas.name: nextbike
schemas.description: Description is concrete — names the provider, the data types (station locations, availability, system info), the geographic coverage (50+ German networks with examples), and the access model (free, no API key). Very actionable.

schemas.name: neynar
schemas.description: Description specifies provider (Neynar), protocol context (Farcaster, REST), and lists concrete operations (by username/FID/ETH address, casts, feeds). Clear enough to route to.

schemas.name: nhtsaRecalls
schemas.description: Description is highly specific — names provider (NHTSA), scope (US recalls + complaints), data dimensions (make/model/year, campaign), and includes the agency full name. Very easy to route to.

schemas.name: nhtsaVpic
schemas.description: Description is exemplary — names provider (NHTSA vPIC), expands acronym, lists primary capability (VIN decoding), data dimensions (make/model/manufacturer), and access constraints (no API key, US scope).

schemas.name: nihreporter
schemas.description: Description clearly names provider context (NIH-funded research), data types (projects, publications, clinical studies) and metadata (grants, PIs, funding). Could specify the agency (NIH RePORTER) by name.

schemas.name: ninaWarnApp
schemas.description: Description specifies provider (NINA Warn-App), data sources (BBK, DWD, KATWARN, BIWAPP, LHP flood), country (Germany), and the type of data (civil protection warnings).

schemas.name: warnings
schemas.description: Description names the federal system (NINA), data types (DWD weather, MOWAS civil protection, BIWAPP, KATWARN), and the country context (German federal warning system). Slightly less detailed than ninaWarnApp counterpart.

schemas.name: noaaWeatherAlerts
schemas.description: Description names the agency (NWS, NOAA), the API (Weather.gov), the country scope (US), and three concrete data dimensions (alerts by state, forecast by grid, station observations). Clear and actionable.

schemas.name: noaa-climate
schemas.description: Exemplary description — names provider (NOAA NCEI), data types (daily, global, normals), coverage (10,000+ stations worldwide), parameters measured (temperature, precipitation, wind), and access constraints (free without key).

schemas.name: nobel-prize
schemas.description: Description names the official source (Nobel Foundation), time range (1901-present), search dimensions (name/gender/country), all six categories, and access model (free, no key). Comprehensive.

schemas.name: nominatim
schemas.description: Description names both capabilities (geocode + reverse geocode) and the data source (OpenStreetMap). Slightly short — does not name the service (Nominatim) explicitly or mention rate-limit / usage policy.

schemas.name: nvdCve
schemas.description: Description names provider (NIST NVD), data types (CVE records, severity scores, affected products, change history) and is concise. Could mention rate-limits or API key behavior.

schemas.name: nwsWeather
schemas.description: Description is highly specific — names agency (US NWS), data dimensions (forecasts, alerts, observations), station count (3000+), geographic scope (entire US), and access model (free, no key).

schemas.name: obis
schemas.description: Description names the system (Ocean Biodiversity Information System), data types (occurrences, taxonomy, checklists, dataset metadata), and scale (100M+ records worldwide). Clear and actionable.

schemas.name: tenders
schemas.description: Description names the service (German Bekanntmachungsservice), data format (OCDS), and key fields (buyer, title, CPV, location). Slightly bilingual which is fine, but no scope (federal/state) or coverage info.

schemas.name: olhcv-moralis-solana
schemas.description: Description names provider (Moralis), data type (OHLCV), chain (Solana), and the recursive auto-pagination feature. Clear, parallel to the EVM variant.

schemas.name: olhcv-solana-tracker
schemas.description: Description names provider (Solana Tracker), data type (OHLCV candlestick chart), scope (Solana token + pool), and use case (price history visualization). Clear and specific.

schemas.name: olhcv-moralis-evm
schemas.description: Description names provider (Moralis), data type (OHLCV candlesticks), scope (EVM token pairs), and notable feature (recursive auto-pagination). Could mention key requirement explicitly.

schemas.name: omdb
schemas.description: Schema description is clear and specific about searching/retrieving movie, TV series, and episode information with examples of returned fields (ratings, plot, cast, poster). Slightly generic but adequate.

schemas.name: openaire
schemas.description: Description is specific with scale (170M+ products), entity types (publications, datasets, software, projects), and key filters (EU funding, open access, impact metrics). Clear when to use.

schemas.name: openalex
schemas.description: Description is specific with scale (240M+) and clearly lists entity types (works, authors, institutions, funders, topics). Communicates open and free nature.

schemas.name: open-brewery-db
schemas.description: Very specific description with filter dimensions (name, city, state, country, type), return fields (address, coordinates, phone, website), and data scale (8,000+ US breweries). Clear free/no-key statement.

schemas.name: opencellid
schemas.description: Description clearly states the three main use cases (lookup, geolocation, area query) and the data scope. Specific to cell tower locations with worldwide scope.

schemas.name: openchargemap
schemas.description: Specific scope (300K+ EV charging stations), feature list (connector types, charging speeds), data origin (crowdsourced) and key data points listed. Clear scope and use case.

schemas.name: opencitations
schemas.description: Clear scope of citation data with specific query types (DOI-to-DOI links, references, counts, metadata) and licensing info (CC0). Well-targeted description.

schemas.name: openfda
schemas.description: Clear scope of FDA datasets covered (drugs, devices, food, recalls, adverse events). Specifies clear use cases. Could mention search syntax.

schemas.name: openfec
schemas.description: Very specific description: scope (FEC campaign finance), entity types (candidates, committees, filings, totals), time range (1980-present), elections (presidential, Senate, House), and access info (API key, DEMO_KEY).

schemas.name: openfigi
schemas.description: Very specific description with identifier types (ISIN, CUSIP, SEDOL, ticker), three use cases (map, search, retrieve values), and access info (free, no key). Clear use cases.

schemas.name: openFoodFacts
schemas.description: Very specific description with database scale (3M+ items), return data types (nutrition, ingredients, allergens, Nutri-Score, eco-scores), and access methods (barcode, category, grade). Clear use cases.

schemas.name: openfootball
schemas.description: Description briefly mentions key leagues (Bundesliga, Premier League, La Liga) and data source (GitHub static JSON). Lacks scope details on coverage (history, granularity).

schemas.name: openLegalData
schemas.description: Description is specific with concrete dataset scale (250K+ decisions, 57K+ law sections, 1100+ law books), data type (German legal data), licensing (CC0). Clear scope.

schemas.name: openLibrary
schemas.description: Description specifies scale (20M+ editions, works, authors, subjects) and source (Internet Archive). Clear scope but could mention what data is queryable.

schemas.name: openligadb
schemas.description: Description mentions key data types (matches, standings, scorers, teams) and language scope (German + international). Could elaborate on supported leagues.

schemas.name: openMeteoAirQuality
schemas.description: Very specific description listing exact pollutants (PM2.5, PM10, ozone, NO2, CO, UV index) and AQI types (European/US). Worldwide scope and time granularity (hourly) clear.

schemas.name: openplz
schemas.description: Specific description with entity types (postal codes, localities, streets, municipalities, admin divisions) and three country scopes (DE/AT/CH). Clear no-auth open data.

schemas.name: openrouteservice
schemas.description: Description lists six distinct services (routing, isochrones, matrix, geocoding, elevation, optimization) and data source (OpenStreetMap). Clear and broad scope.

schemas.name: opensanctions
schemas.description: Clear use cases (screen persons/companies, retrieve entities, dataset metadata) and data types (sanctions, PEP, criminal records). API name (yente) specified.

schemas.name: opensea
schemas.description: Clear scope (NFT marketplace data) with specific data types (collection stats, listings, offers, events, metadata) and version (v2 API). Includes tool selection guidance.

schemas.name: opensensemap
schemas.description: Specific description with network scale (16K+ stations), measurements (air quality, temperature, humidity, UV, noise), and provenance (re:edu, University of Muenster). Worldwide scope.

schemas.name: openskyNetwork
schemas.description: Very specific: real-time aircraft tracking, four data types (state vectors, flight history, arrivals/departures, waypoints), and data source (ADS-B receiver network).

schemas.name: openStates
schemas.description: Clear geographic scope (all 50 states, DC, Puerto Rico) and entity types (legislators, bills, committees, votes). Concise and well-targeted.

schemas.name: opentdb
schemas.description: Very specific: 4,000+ questions, 24 categories, question types (multiple choice, true/false), difficulty levels, example categories, access info (free, no API key). Excellent specificity.

schemas.name: opsd
schemas.description: Very specific: 6000+ plants, 20+ European countries, scope (conventional power), filter dimensions, energy types (fossil, renewable, nuclear), and access (free, no API key).

schemas.name: orca
schemas.description: Description is clear and specific — names Orca DEX on Solana, lists exactly what data is fetched (pool data, token listings, whirlpool details), and gives explicit usage guidance with tool chaining.

schemas.name: orcid
schemas.description: Description is concrete with scale (19M+ profiles), enumerates content types (publications, affiliations, fundings, peer reviews), and notes auth/cost — gives LLM clear trigger conditions.

schemas.name: pangaea
schemas.description: Description names PANGAEA, scale (400K+ datasets), domain (earth/environmental/climate/ocean/geoscience), and access pattern (georeferenced datasets). Clear scope for LLM trigger.

schemas.name: onchain-data
schemas.description: Description identifies Passport scores/credentials/attestations and networks (Ethereum, Arbitrum) but is brief — could benefit from explaining 'Passport' (Gitcoin Passport sybil resistance) context.

schemas.name: pegelonline
schemas.description: Description names Pegelonline, scope (German federal waterways), data type (real-time water levels, station data, measurements) and access (no API key) — clear trigger conditions.

schemas.name: water-levels
schemas.description: Description names domain (German federal waterway gauging stations), data type (water levels, measurements, station data) and source (WSV). Slightly less explicit than the sister schema.

schemas.name: pesticides
schemas.description: Description identifies source (German BVL), scope (pesticide registration database), and content categories (products, ingredients, companies, restrictions) — clear and specific trigger.

schemas.name: read
schemas.description: Description clearly identifies Pinata as IPFS gateway, access pattern (by CID), and auth requirement. Concise and specific.

schemas.name: graphql
schemas.description: Description identifies POAP protocol, GraphQL access, and three usage modes (introspection, predefined queries, custom). Could elaborate slightly on POAP context (Proof of Attendance Protocol).

schemas.name: podcastIndex
schemas.description: Description gives scale (4M+ podcasts) and concrete capabilities (search, trending, recent, live, categories). Clear, specific trigger conditions.

schemas.name: pokeapi
schemas.description: Description is exemplary — names PokeAPI, scale (1,300+ Pokemon), capabilities (stats, abilities, types, moves, evolution chains), generation coverage, additional data (berries, items, locations), and access (free, no key).

schemas.name: marketInfo
schemas.description: Description names Polymarket CLOB API, scope (prediction markets), and two operations (list with filters, retrieve by condition ID). Clear and specific.

schemas.name: searchBySlug
schemas.description: Description is concise and concrete — names Polymarket, Gamma API, lookup mechanism (URL slug), and return structure (embedded markets, outcomes, probabilities).

schemas.name: gammaApi
schemas.description: Description identifies Polymarket Gamma API and capabilities (search events, list events, list markets) but is brief and the 'readable, table-like summaries' phrasing is implementation detail rather than user-facing scope.

schemas.name: profileJump
schemas.description: Description names ProfileJump for LUKSO Universal Profiles and lists capabilities (token prices, trending profiles, listings, profile lookup). Clear scope.

schemas.name: pubchem
schemas.description: Description is exemplary — names PubChem, source (NCBI/NIH), scale (115M+ compounds), search modes (name/formula/structure), property types, content categories (drugs, metabolites, toxins, etc.), and access.

schemas.name: pumpfun
schemas.description: Description names Pump.fun on Solana, content scope (meme token launches, coins, live streams, replies, creator profiles), and gives explicit chaining hint (getCoins → getCoinByMint).

schemas.name: pvgis
schemas.description: Description names PVGIS by JRC, scope (solar PV energy output, radiation, time series, horizon profiles), and abbreviation expansion (EU Photovoltaic Geographical Information System). Clear trigger.

schemas.name: charts
schemas.description: Description is concise — identifies QuickChart.io for PNG generation from Chart.js configs, no API key. Could clarify the difference between renderChart and getChartUrl modes.

schemas.name: rcsb-pdb
schemas.description: Description is exemplary — names RCSB PDB, scope (worldwide 3D macromolecule archive), scale (220,000+ structures), data types (proteins, nucleic acids, complexes), returned content (atomic coordinates, experimental details), and access (free).

schemas.name: regionalatlas
schemas.description: Description gives scale (160+ indicators), regional granularity (federal states, districts, Kreise), and topical coverage (population, demographics, economy, education, environment). Clear trigger.

schemas.name: regionalstatistik
schemas.description: Description names source (Regionaldatenbank Deutschland via GENESIS REST API), scale (160+ indicators), regional granularity (Kreis/Gemeinde/Bundesland), and auth note (free registration). Clear trigger.

schemas.name: reisewarnung
schemas.description: Description names source (German Federal Foreign Office / Auswaertiges Amt) and content (travel advisories, country security info, diplomatic contacts). Clear and specific.

schemas.name: travel-warnings
schemas.description: Description names source (German Federal Foreign Office / Auswaertiges Amt) and scope (travel warnings, safety advisories, worldwide). Concise and clear.

schemas.name: rest-countries
schemas.description: Description is specific with concrete data points (250+ countries) and lists key data fields (names, capitals, currencies, languages, flags, geographic data). Clear scope, no ambiguity.

schemas.name: retractionwatch
schemas.description: Concrete scope (63K+ articles), specifies access path (Crossref API), lists all filter dimensions (date, DOI, publisher, keyword), and clarifies data source.

schemas.name: rickandmorty
schemas.description: Concrete counts (800+ characters, 50+ episodes, 120+ locations), explicit filter dimensions, and clear statement about free access without API key.

schemas.name: rijksmuseum
schemas.description: Clear scope (700K+ objects) with filter dimensions listed and access requirement (no API key). Concise but lacks examples of typical use cases.

schemas.name: rki-covid
schemas.description: Clearly identifies source (RKI) and lists data granularities (national, state, district, vaccination, testing). No counts but explicit dimensions.

schemas.name: ror
schemas.description: Concrete scale (100,000+ organizations), clear capability statement (search/retrieve/match), and identifies the data source (ROR). Could elaborate on use cases.

schemas.name: tokenSafety
schemas.description: Identifies Solana scope and key capabilities (safety scores, risk reports, votes, trending). Source (rugcheck.xyz) noted. Lacks concrete metrics or counts.

schemas.name: rxnorm
schemas.description: Identifies source (NLM RxNorm) and lists key data types (drug concepts, RxCUI, NDC codes, properties, related medications). Clear domain coverage.

schemas.name: transaction-service
schemas.description: Clear scope (Gnosis Safe multisig wallets) with key data dimensions (balances, transactions, owners, modules) and chain context (Ethereum mainnet). Concise.

schemas.name: sec-data
schemas.description: Detailed scope (SEC EDGAR XBRL, all US public companies), specifies CIK format with concrete example (CIK0000320193), notes free access and User-Agent header requirement.

schemas.name: sec-edgar
schemas.description: Specific time range (1993 to present), explicit form examples (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, S-1), search dimensions noted, and access requirements documented.

schemas.name: semanticscholar
schemas.description: Concrete scale (200M+ papers), specific features listed (TLDR summaries, citation intent, embeddings, author profiles), and access info documented.

schemas.name: sensorcommunity
schemas.description: Identifies source with former name (Sensor.Community formerly Luftdaten.info), lists measured variables (PM2.5, PM10, temp, humidity) and scope (thousands of DIY sensors worldwide).

schemas.name: serlo
schemas.description: Identifies platform (Serlo.org OER), protocol (GraphQL), and capabilities (subjects, articles, exercises, taxonomy, activity). German context noted, no API key required.

schemas.name: tenders
schemas.description: Identifies content (German Ausschreibungen and awarded contracts), scope (federal, state, municipal), and access method (RSS feed). Bilingual terms aid German users.

schemas.name: shodaninternetdb
schemas.description: Clear capability list (open ports, CPEs, hostnames, tags, CVEs) and clear access info (no API key). Concrete IP-based use case.

schemas.name: balancesEVM
schemas.description: Clear data scope (native + ERC20 tokens with USD valuations) and realtime nature across EVM chains. Concise.

schemas.name: balancesSVM
schemas.description: Token types listed (native, SPL, SPL-2022) with USD valuation, scope (SVM chains) clear. Concise.

schemas.name: collectiblesEVM
schemas.description: Specifies NFT standards (ERC721, ERC1155), data returned (metadata, images, collection details), and scope (EVM chains).

schemas.name: tokenInfoEVM
schemas.description: Clear list of metadata fields (symbol, name, decimals, USD price, logo URLs), and scope (native + ERC20 across EVM chains).

schemas.name: transactionsEVM
schemas.description: Describes data scope (block info, gas, raw values) and ordering. Lacks explicit mention of EVM scope in description but inferable from schema name.

schemas.name: activityEVM
schemas.description: Clear activity types listed (transfers, swaps, mints, burns, approvals) and scope (EVM chains, realtime). Decoded format noted.

schemas.name: tokenHoldersEVM
schemas.description: Briefly describes token holder distribution scope for ERC20/ERC721. Lacks scale or detail compared to siblings. Mentions ranking by wallet value.

schemas.name: transactionsSVM
schemas.description: Describes SVM transaction history with block info and ordering. Brief but accurate. Could note specific data fields returned.

schemas.name: energy
schemas.description: Beschreibt klar die Datenquelle (deutscher Strommarkt von SMARD/Bundesnetzagentur) und Datentypen, bleibt aber relativ generisch ohne konkrete Use Cases.

schemas.name: smithsonianOpenAccess
schemas.description: Klare Datenquelle (Smithsonian Open Access, 11M+ Objekte) mit konkretem Scope (Museumsobjekte, Kunst, Artefakte). Use Cases bleiben implizit aber gut ableitbar.

schemas.name: smithsonianVolcanoes
schemas.description: Praezise Beschreibung (GVP/Smithsonian Volcanoes via WFS, Holocene/Pleistocene, GeoJSON). Macht klar was zurueckkommt und in welchem Format.

schemas.name: soilgrids
schemas.description: Klare Beschreibung (ISRIC SoilGrids, 250m Aufloesung, WRB-Klassifikation, multiple Tiefen). Erkennbar fuer welche Use Cases nutzbar (Bodenkunde, Landwirtschaft).

schemas.name: --priceEndpoints
schemas.description: Sehr praezise Beschreibung listet alle Preis-Modi (real-time, historisch, timestamp, ranges, batch multi-token) auf. Provider klar genannt.

schemas.name: --chartRoutes
schemas.description: Klare Beschreibung (OHLCV-Candles, Holder-Distribution, Solana Tracker, Tokens und Pools). Use Case ist sofort erkennbar.

schemas.name: --pnlRoutes
schemas.description: Sehr klare Beschreibung (PnL-Analytik fuer Solana Wallets via Solana Tracker, drei Sub-Funktionen: voll, per-Token, first-buyer).

schemas.name: --tradeEndpoints
schemas.description: Praezise (DEX Trades, by token/pool/wallet/combination, Pagination, Jupiter parse mode). Use Cases klar.

schemas.name: --walletEndpoints
schemas.description: Klar (Wallet-Daten via Solana Tracker, Token-Balances mit USD-Werten, Trades, paginierte Portfolio-Views).

schemas.name: --topTraderRoutes
schemas.description: Klar (top-profitable Traders, Pagination, optional token-specific). Knapp aber praezise.

schemas.name: getChainInfo
schemas.description: Klar (Solscan, allgemeine Solana-Chain-Info: Block height, TX count, TPS, supply). Sehr eng gefasste, klar erkennbare Funktion.

schemas.name: analysis
schemas.description: Klar (SolSniffer Token-Security-Analyse, Risk-Assessment, Rug-Pull, Mint Authority, Safety Score). Konkrete Anwendung erkennbar.

schemas.name: verification
schemas.description: Klar aber knapp (Sourcify, Smart-Contract Source Verification, multi-chain). Use Cases (verification status) erkennbar.

schemas.name: spaceid
schemas.description: Praezise (SPACE ID on-chain Resolution fuer Domains und Adressen). Kompakt aber klar.

schemas.name: spacexApi
schemas.description: Klar und vollstaendig (Launches, Rockets, Crew, Starlink, Launchpads, Company via open SpaceX API). Use Cases offensichtlich.

schemas.name: starknet-token
schemas.description: Praezise (ERC20 Balances und Block-Info auf Starknet via public RPC, kein API-Key). Klar abgegrenzt.

schemas.name: memorial-stones
schemas.description: Klar (Stolpersteine in Berlin, Holocaust-Gedenkstaetten). Use Cases (Recherche, Bildung) implizit erkennbar.

schemas.name: radiation
schemas.description: Sehr praezise (BfS Strahlenschutz, ambient dose rate, WFS/OGC, 1685 Stationen). Quelle und Datenmenge klar.

schemas.name: api-registry
schemas.description: Klar (SwaggerHub Registry-API, Search, Metadata, Swagger-Definitionen). Use Cases offensichtlich fuer API-Konsumenten.

schemas.name: news
schemas.description: Klar (ARD tagesschau, Headlines, Suche, Livestreams). Provider und Use Case klar erkennbar.

schemas.name: procurement
schemas.description: Klar (TED, EU-Procurement Notices, Expert-Query-Syntax). Wichtiger Use Case (EU-Vergaben) klar erkennbar.

schemas.name: public-contracts
schemas.description: Klar (Tenderly, public smart contract info, source verification, compiler, token metadata). Use Case eindeutig.

schemas.name: getSchema
schemas.description: Description clearly states purpose (GraphQL schemas + arbitrary queries on The Graph subgraphs). Slightly brief but specific enough to know when to use.

schemas.name: tle-api
schemas.description: Description is specific (NORAD TLE orbital data, ISS, weather satellites) and conveys clear use cases. Concise and unambiguous.

schemas.name: trading-signals-momentum
schemas.description: Clear scope (momentum indicators), explicit indicator list (RSI, MACD, etc.), and notes local computation. Very informative for tool selection.

schemas.name: trading-signals-ohlc
schemas.description: Description specifies OHLC requirement and lists indicators (ADX, ATR, CCI, etc.). Helpful disambiguation from price-only indicators.

schemas.name: trading-signals-trend
schemas.description: Description clearly scopes trend-following indicators, names all variants, and emphasizes local computation. Disambiguates from momentum/volume schemas.

schemas.name: trading-signals-volatility
schemas.description: Clearly scopes volatility indicators with all four members enumerated and notes local computation. Concise and specific.

schemas.name: trading-signals-volume
schemas.description: Description clearly scopes volume+candle indicators, lists members (OBV, VWAP, AC, PSAR, ZigZag), and notes local computation.

schemas.name: transitousMotis
schemas.description: Highly specific description: pan-European transit, 30+ countries, modes listed, free, no key. Perfect for routing decisions.

schemas.name: transportRestVbb
schemas.description: Description is highly specific: Berlin-Brandenburg VBB, lists all modes (S-Bahn, U-Bahn, tram, bus, regional), all functions (search, departures, journeys, vehicle radar, reachable stops). Free, no key.

schemas.name: transportRestVbbExtended
schemas.description: Description lists complementary VBB functions (nearby stops, trip search, line info, shapes, autocomplete) and notes it complements the core schema. Clear scope.

schemas.name: tvmaze
schemas.description: Description states scope (70k+ shows, episodes, cast, schedules), API freeness, and use cases. Highly specific.

schemas.name: uk-parliament
schemas.description: Description specifies UK Parliament Members API, search dimensions (name/party/constituency/House), data returned, scope (both Houses, current+historical), free, no key. Excellent clarity.

schemas.name: un-comtrade
schemas.description: Excellent description: scope (200+ countries, 1962-present, monthly/annual), classification (HS), preview limitation, rate limit. Highly specific for selection.

schemas.name: unescoWorldHeritage
schemas.description: Description clearly conveys 1200+ heritage sites with filterable dimensions (country, category, danger, inscription year). Free, public. Specific and concise.

schemas.name: unified-momentum-advanced
schemas.description: Clear scope: advanced momentum indicators with multi-library selection (indicatorts/trading-signals/talib). Lists 8 indicators. Useful for picking when basic momentum is insufficient.

schemas.name: unified-momentum-basic
schemas.description: Description states basic momentum scope, multi-library selection, lists 8 indicators (RSI, MACD, Stochastic etc.), and local computation note. Clear differentiation from advanced variant.

schemas.name: unified-trend-advanced
schemas.description: Description clearly scopes advanced trend indicators (DMA, KAMA, MAMA, T3, Linear Regression family) with library selection (trading-signals/talib). Local computation noted.

schemas.name: unified-trend-basic
schemas.description: Description clearly scopes basic moving averages (SMA, EMA, DEMA, WMA, TEMA, TRIMA, WSMA, RMA) with multi-library selection. Clear differentiation from advanced trend.

schemas.name: unified-trend-channels
schemas.description: Description clearly scopes trend channels/bands (Bollinger, Keltner, Donchian, Acceleration Bands, Ichimoku) with multi-library selection. Specific membership listing.

schemas.name: unified-volatility
schemas.description: Clear scope: volatility indicators (ATR, TR, NATR, IQR, MAD, Ulcer Index, Mass Index, Chandelier Exit) with multi-library selection. Local computation noted.

schemas.name: unified-volume
schemas.description: Clear scope: volume-based indicators (OBV, VWAP, AD, ADOSC, CMF, MFI, VWMA, VPT) with multi-library selection. Local computation noted.

schemas.name: uniprot
schemas.description: Description specifies UniProt scope (250M+ entries, sequences/functions/classifications/cross-refs). Concise and clearly delimits the protein-data use case.

schemas.name: price-discovery
schemas.description: Description is brief: price discovery via Uniswap V3 with CryptoCompare fallback. Adequate but lacks scope details (supported chains, fee tier, return format).

schemas.name: uniswap-pool-explorer
schemas.description: Description states Uniswap V2/V3/V4 pools via The Graph subgraphs, but is brief and lacks detail on data returned.

schemas.name: unpaywall
schemas.description: Description is precise: detects OA status, finds free PDFs by DOI, 100M+ articles, lists OA classes (gold/green/hybrid/bronze). Clear scope.

schemas.name: us-congress
schemas.description: Description is specific and clear: names data source (Library of Congress), enumerates entity types (bills, members, committees, etc.), specifies coverage (93rd Congress to present), and includes auth note (DEMO_KEY).

schemas.name: usdaFoodData
schemas.description: Description is clear and specific: names provider (USDA FoodData Central) and lists supported actions (search, nutrient details, browse). Could mention auth requirement explicitly.

schemas.name: usgsEarthquake
schemas.description: Description names provider and core capabilities (search by location/magnitude/date/depth, GeoJSON). Clear and specific but compact.

schemas.name: transit
schemas.description: Description is specific: city (Nuremberg/VAG), data type (real-time stops/departures/service info), modes (bus, tram, subway). Compact and informative.

schemas.name: vanda
schemas.description: Specific provider (Victoria and Albert Museum), scope (1M+ objects, 5000 years). Clear and informative.

schemas.name: vrrEfa
schemas.description: Description is specific: region (Rhein-Ruhr), broader scope (Germany-wide via DELFI), capabilities (stops, departures, trip planning, alerts), auth (no key required).

schemas.name: wegweiser-kommune
schemas.description: Exemplary description: names provider (Bertelsmann Stiftung), scope (100+ indicators, 5000+ residents), data categories, year range (2006-2040), license (CC0), auth (none). Highly specific.

schemas.name: whogho
schemas.description: Description is specific: provider (WHO GHO), scope (indicators, country stats, dimensions), API style (OData). Clear and concise.

schemas.name: wikidata
schemas.description: Specific provider (Wikidata), scope (100M+ items, structured knowledge), interface (SPARQL + predefined queries), auth (no key required). Comprehensive overview.

schemas.name: wikimediaCommons
schemas.description: Specific: provider (Wikimedia Commons), scope (100M+ files), capabilities (search, browse, metadata, license/author), licensing (CC). Clear and complete.

schemas.name: wikipedia
schemas.description: Specific: provider (Wikipedia), capabilities (search, summary, OpenSearch), API types (REST + Action), no key required. Clear and complete.

schemas.name: wipoipc
schemas.description: Specific: provider (WIPO IPCPUB), purpose (IPC symbol validity, classification details), and output fields described. Clear and focused.

schemas.name: worldBank
schemas.description: Specific: provider (World Bank), scope (17,000+ indicators, 260+ countries), example domains (GDP, population, poverty, health).

schemas.name: wormholescan
schemas.description: Specific: provider (Wormholescan), domain (cross-chain bridge metrics), capabilities (activity, money flow, top assets/pairs/corridors, KPIs).

schemas.name: chart
schemas.description: Specific: provider (Yahoo Finance), data (OHLCV), generality (any ticker), no API key. Clear and concise.

schemas.name: zdb
schemas.description: Specific: provider (Zeitschriftendatenbank/ZDB), scope (2.2M titles, 24.7M holdings, 3700+ libraries), output format (JSON-LD), no key required.

schemas.name: zenodo
schemas.description: Specific: provider (Zenodo/CERN), scope (research datasets, publications, software). Clear, though lacks mention of OAI/scale.

schemas.name: zerox
schemas.description: Specific: provider (0x Protocol v2), domain (DEX aggregator/swaps), scope (100+ sources, 20+ chains), approval flows (Allowance Holder, Permit2). Comprehensive.

schemas.name: auctions
schemas.description: Specific: provider (zvg-portal.de), scope (foreclosure auctions all 16 German states), data types (auction dates, property details, valuations, court info).

schemas.name: zwangsversteigerungen
schemas.description: Specific provider (official ZVG portal of German courts) and domain (foreclosure auctions), but compact and lacks detail on coverage or available filters.
